title: phaulopsis barteri var. pauciglandula
intro: Phaulopsis barteri var. pauciglandula, also known as the African snake flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in West African medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Phaulopsis barteri var. pauciglandula include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture or decoction, where the leaves and stems are steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
